title: alchemilla hybrida subsp. helvetica
intro: Alchemilla hybrida subsp. helvetica, also known as lady's mantle or lady's thumb, is a perennial plant species within the Alchemilla genus, known for its attractive, chartreuse flowers and deeply lobed leaves. The plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Alchemilla hybrida subsp. helvetica include reducing menstrual cramps, soothing skin irritations, and acting as an antispasmodic agent to alleviate digestive issues. Alchemilla hybrida subsp. helvetica can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in herbal remedies to support women's health and well-being.
